rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference

Find co-expression cliques using C++ two-level decomposition

Source: R/cliques.R
find_cliques.Rd

For each Hierarchical Ortholog Group (HOG), uses Bron-Kerbosch with pivoting on the species-level adjacency graph to find maximal species cliques, then backtracking to assign the best gene per species (minimising mean q-value across all present edges).

find_cliques(
  edges,
  target_species,
  min_species = length(target_species),
  max_genes_per_sp = 10L,
  max_missing_edges = 0L,
  edge_type = "conserved"
)

Arguments

edges

Data frame with columns:

gene1

Gene identifier (first gene in pair)

gene2

Gene identifier (second gene in pair)

species1

Species for gene1

species2

Species for gene2

hog

Ortholog group identifier

q.value

q-value for the edge (from pair-level testing)

effect_size

Numeric effect size

Optionally includes a type column for filtering.

target_species

Character vector of species abbreviations.

min_species

Minimum number of species per clique (default: length(target_species)).

max_genes_per_sp

Maximum genes considered per species per HOG (default 10). Keeps the most-connected genes.

max_missing_edges

Maximum number of missing species-pair edges tolerated per clique (default 0 = all edges required). When > 0, uses subset enumeration instead of Bron-Kerbosch.

edge_type

If edges has a type column, keep only edges with type %in% edge_type (default "conserved").

Value

A data frame with one row per clique:

hog

Ortholog group identifier

One column per target species (gene ID or NA)

n_species

Number of species in the clique

mean_q

Mean q-value across present clique edges

max_q

Maximum q-value across present clique edges

mean_effect_size

Mean effect size across present edges

n_edges

Number of present edges

n_missing

Number of missing edges (0 when max_missing_edges = 0)

Details

When max_missing_edges > 0, tolerates up to that many missing species-pair edges per clique. Instead of Bron-Kerbosch (which only finds fully connected subgraphs), all species subsets with at most max_missing_edges missing edges are enumerated. Assignments prefer fewer missing edges, then lower mean q-value.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
cliques <- find_cliques(edges, target_species = c("SP_A", "SP_B", "SP_C"))

# Allow partial species cliques (2 of 3 species)
partial <- find_cliques(edges, target_species, min_species = 2L)

# Tolerate 1 missing edge (e.g., 5 of 6 edges in a 4-species clique)
tolerant <- find_cliques(edges, target_species, max_missing_edges = 1L)
} # }

Contents
